# ADR-005: PHP 8.0 Minimum, PHP 7 Dropped in v4.0.0

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

As of 2022, PHP 7.0, 7.1, 7.2, 7.3, and 7.4 had all reached end-of-life or were approaching it. v3.x of this library supported PHP 7.4+. The v4.0.0 release needed to make breaking changes to `ParserInterface` (locale-aware parsing, see ADR-003) and presented a natural opportunity to drop legacy PHP support.

PHP 8.0 introduced named arguments, union types, `match` expressions, and `str_contains()` — features that improve code clarity. PHP 8.1+ adds fibers, enums, and readonly properties. Maintaining PHP 7 support constrains the language features available to both this library and its contributors.

## Decision

v4.0.0 dropped support for PHP 7.x entirely. The `composer.json` minimum PHP version is `^8.0`. CI tests against PHP 8.0, 8.1, 8.2, 8.3, and 8.4.

## Consequences

- Applications still on PHP 7 must stay on `contentful/rich-text` v3.x.
- PHP 8's type system and syntax improvements are available — `declare(strict_types=1)` is used in all source files.
- PHP 8.2 had a known issue causing PHPUnit tests to segfault (noted in the CI config comment) — this was a PHP bug, not a library bug, and was resolved in subsequent PHP patch releases.
- PHP 8.4 support was added in a 2024 CI update.
